pundit.name: Clint Dempsey
pundit.take: **Gayden's midfield is lookin' shakier than Rafe's soccer knowledge.** We've got Turkey, led by The Backpass Assassin himself, Gayden, goin' up against the USA under Ghost Pepper, Devin. Turkey's been strugglin' to keep opponents out, but Gayden drafted 'em like he's got a secret Turkish recipe for success hidden in his basement or somethin’. Meanwhile, Ghost Pepper's USA knows how to grind out a result — a couple of 'em might even be puttin' in work like Texas BBQ. I reckon the USA's takin' this one easy like a Sunday morning. Sorry, Gayden, but it looks like you’re about to add another loss to that stack — maybe time to back yourself with some teams that actually, y’know, win games.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach Fails to Muster Tactics as Mexico Stomps Czechia
pundit_takes.subtitle: Loss questions Mustard Boy's strategy, and possibly his condiment choices.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Backpass Strategy Under Fire After South Korea Defeat
pundit_takes.subtitle: Backpass Assassin may need to make forward pass adjustments.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Set to Spice Things Up Against Gayden’s Turkey
pundit_takes.subtitle: Ghost Pepper poised to remind Turkey they aren't Thanksgiving dinner.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Maintains Distant Fourth as Father Gayden Struggles in Fifth
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker managing to stay ahead of his own father.

rome_analytics_quotes: Gunner's 28% win probability just ran into a teenage wrecking ball named Rafe.
Rafe drafting three non-qualifiers is peak comedy—still chilling in 4th.
Rafe and Gayden locked at 0 differential—this family dinner's gonna need a referee.
Devin's odds: predicted second, but still holds top spot. Sim's got some 'splaining to do.
